action: Batch-scoped per-CLI circuit breaker: after N (default 2) rate_limit escalations from one CLI family within a batch, demote that family for the remainder of the batch (route phases directly to their fallback CLI) until a cheap probe succeeds or the batch ends. Evidence: cycle 283 — codex daily quota wall at 2026-06-11T00:28Z; EVERY codex-routed phase (api-contract-design, architecture-design, test-amplification) exited 85 (ExitUnknownPrompt via rate_limit pattern, bridge/exitcodes.go:32) and burned its full boot + stall-detection window (5-15 min each) before the claude fallback ran. The dispatch chain works but wastes ~30-45 min/cycle and turn budget while a CLI is walled. State could live in .evolve/state.json currentBatch (cliHealth map) consulted by llmroute before each dispatch (llmroute.go defaultFallbackOnExit already enumerates 85).
evidence: evolve-batch2.log dispatch chains codex-tmux=85 -> claude-tmux=0 x3
